affordances.name: Distill the core with boundaries
affordances.mechanism: When complexity blocks action, alignment, or recall, simplify to the essential governing idea while preserving the caveat, boundary, or uncertainty that keeps the simplification safe to use.

use_when: Complexity is blocking action, alignment, or recall more than improving decision quality.
The audience needs the essential governing idea rather than every analysis branch.
A recommendation must survive handoff to operators who need a rule, intent, or checkpoint.
do_not_use_when: The simplification removes the caveat, boundary, or uncertainty that makes the decision safe.
The source problem is novel enough that a previous simplified frame may mislead.
The output would be accurate but useless for prediction, decision, or action.
case_evidence_needed: The complexity or clutter blocking action.
The core decision, action, or message that should survive.
The boundary condition that must not be removed.
The audience or operator who must use the simplification.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to name one core idea and one safety boundary, avoiding both clutter and false simplicity.
evidence_required: The decision or handoff that needs simplification.
The main point or governing idea.
The critical caveat, boundary, or uncertainty.
The user or operator action the simplification should enable.
good_output_shape: States the core in plain language.
Cuts details that do not advance the purpose.
Makes the next action or decision easier.

diagnostic_questions: What is the one idea that must survive the handoff?
Which caveat would make this unsafe if removed?
Is this simpler in a way that helps someone decide or act?
misuse_guards: Do not confuse simplification with dumbing down.
Do not remove uncertainty that the downstream actor needs.
Do not keep an accurate statement if it is operationally useless.

source_evidence.source_quote: ruthless prioritization and distillation of information to its essential core
removes the very caveat, boundary, or uncertainty
not merely brevity but achieving "useful simplicity"

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports simplification as core-plus-boundary discipline.
dropped_material: Did not extract generic shortness or slogan advice.
Did not collapse simplification into narrative or UX heuristics.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card helps keep packets compact without losing safety caveats.

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly says simplification is not merely making things easy; it is useful simplicity that preserves decision value.

source_evidence.source_quote: not merely about making things easy

absence_records.reason: The source requires finding the core and preserving usefulness; a simple message without the relevant trade-off is not supported.

source_evidence.source_quote: If the audience remembers only one thing
complexity is blocking action, alignment, or recall

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 source reading found strong reviewed depth for simplification as actionable compression with boundary protection.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A later packet should test whether simplification helps renderer/card burden decisions.
